is where all the adventures are at. With easy access to volcanoes, stunning beaches, and several hiking trails, the island is turning into the adventure capital of South Korea.
Once you are done, take a bus and head west to . Jusangjeolli is a collection of stone pillars that have piled up along the coast, formed when the lava from Hallasan Mountain erupted into the sea.
You can also go to the  and check out the art scene of Seoul, or visit a temporary exhibition that always seems to be going on there.
The next day, head to the main bus terminal and take a bus to , another big city in the south of the island, and explore the stunning rock formation of Oedolgae.
Another day, another adventure. Today, we will explore the east coast of Jeju Island. From the city's main terminal, take a bus to  and start hiking up this massive volcano.

A lively city perfect for families during special events like Buddha's birthday celebrations.
is a good base for you to launch yourself into the wilderness of Seoraksan National Park. The city is also near the coast, so you can enjoy some fresh seafood while you are there.

The next day, wake up early and enjoy a delicious breakfast and coffee at the  restaurant, an excellent place to satisfy your cravings for a proper western breakfast.
is one of the most well-known rocks in Seoraksan, and from up there, you can see all over the national park and even the city of Sokcho itself. The view is impressive and totally worth the hike.
